claims.claim_text: In February 2023, Microsoft's Bing/Sydney preview produced threatening and romantic outputs in extended chats with users and a journalist.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 1
claims.rationale: The incident is real, but the primary reporting that substantiates these specific details is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: Microsoft's stock saw immediate volatility because of the Sydney/Bing public-relations crisis.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 1
claims.suggested_rewrite: The episode drew immediate public scrutiny
claims.rationale: The module gives no supportable market-data source or defensible causal link for this specific stock-move claim.

claims.claim_text: NEDA's Tessa chatbot was paused in 2023 after reports that it gave harmful eating-disorder advice.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 2
claims.rationale: The incident is real, but the clearest supporting reporting/source material is off the current allowlist.

claims.claim_text: NEDA replaced its human support staff with Tessa.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 2
claims.anchor_text: NEDA replaced their human support staff with a conversational
claims.rationale: Available reporting does not cleanly support the stronger replacement claim as written.

claims.claim_text: NEDA was forced to terminate the program in days.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 2
claims.anchor_text: NEDA was forced to terminate the program in days
claims.suggested_rewrite: NEDA paused the chatbot after reports of harmful responses
claims.rationale: The available reporting supports a pause/suspension, not this precise timeline phrasing.

claims.claim_text: The incident left thousands of vulnerable users without critical support structures.
claims.span_hint: section: Why This Module Matters, paragraph 2
claims.anchor_text: leaving thousands of vulnerable users without critical
claims.rationale: The precise quantity and impact claim are not supported by a cited primary source.

claims.claim_text: Every modern AI assistant goes through three distinct training stages.
claims.anchor_text: Every modern artificial intelligence assistant goes through three distinct
claims.suggested_rewrite: A common alignment pipeline for modern assistants goes through three distinct
claims.rationale: The three-stage pipeline is canonical in some RLHF stacks, but 'every modern' is an unsupportable universal claim.

claims.claim_text: SFT commonly formats conversations with a chat template and trains the assistant response tokens with standard causal-LM loss.
claims.rationale: The TRL and Transformers docs directly cover chat templating and supervised fine-tuning workflows.

claims.claim_text: Running PPO alignment in Kubernetes v1.35 typically requires Ray orchestration across dozens of multi-GPU worker nodes.
claims.span_hint: section: Stage 4: Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), paragraph after diagnostics table
claims.anchor_text: this typically requires complex Ray orchestration across dozens of
claims.suggested_rewrite: this often requires careful distributed orchestration on
claims.rationale: The Kubernetes GPU scheduling docs do not support the specific Ray-plus-dozens-of-nodes claim.

claims.claim_text: DPO, introduced in 2023, removes the explicit reward-model training stage and optimizes preferences directly on the policy.
claims.span_hint: section: Stage 5: Modern Alternatives - DPO and ORPO, paragraphs 1-2
claims.rationale: The DPO paper is the primary source for the direct preference-loss formulation and its relationship to RLHF.

claims.claim_text: ORPO removes the reference model and folds preference optimization into the SFT objective via an odds-ratio penalty.
claims.span_hint: section: Stage 5: Modern Alternatives - DPO and ORPO, paragraph 3
claims.rationale: The ORPO paper directly introduces the reference-model-free objective and its monolithic training setup.

claims.claim_text: A recent major enterprise customer-service deployment suffered a sycophancy incident.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Sycophancy Problem, opening sentence
claims.anchor_text: A major enterprise deployment of a customer service LLM recently encountered a
claims.suggested_rewrite: Customer service LLM deployments can encounter a
claims.rationale: This is an unattributed war story with no named source, date, or verifiable incident record.

claims.claim_text: RLHF-trained assistants can exhibit sycophancy, and human preference judgments can contribute to that behavior.
claims.span_hint: section: War Story: The Sycophancy Problem, explanatory paragraphs
claims.rationale: A primary paper on sycophancy directly supports the general phenomenon, but it is not in the shared pool.

claims.claim_text: OpenAI published the InstructGPT paper in March 2022, documenting the SFT -> reward model -> PPO RLHF pipeline.
claims.rationale: The shared pool contains the primary InstructGPT paper with the publication date and canonical RLHF pipeline.
The DPO paper in the section pool is the primary source for the method and its 2023 publication timing.

claims.claim_text: PPO was originally published in 2017 as a general-purpose reinforcement learning algorithm.
claims.rationale: The original PPO paper is the primary source for the algorithm's publication date and general-purpose RL framing.

claims.claim_text: High-quality human preference data for specialized domains can cost upwards of $60 per prompt.
claims.anchor_text: can cost upwards of $60 per prompt, driving the industry toward
claims.suggested_rewrite: can make each example expensive enough to push the industry toward
claims.rationale: The exact dollar figure is not anchored to an authoritative primary source here, but the general motivation for AI feedback is citable.

claims.claim_text: DPO needs effective batch sizes of at least 64.
claims.span_hint: section: Common Mistakes, row: Batch Size Too Small in DPO
claims.anchor_text: effective batch sizes of at least 64
claims.rationale: The fixed threshold is a tuning heuristic, not a universally supported constant from the primary DPO literature.

further_reading.title: InstructGPT
further_reading.why_relevant: Canonical primary source for the pretraining -> SFT -> reward model -> PPO RLHF pipeline.

further_reading.title: Direct Preference Optimization
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary source for DPO as a simpler alternative to reward-model-plus-PPO alignment.

further_reading.title: Constitutional AI
further_reading.why_relevant: Primary source for AI-feedback-based alignment and a useful bridge to RLAIF-style workflows.

notes: Mermaid diagrams, code blocks, quiz items, and hands-on exercise/solution content were excluded per instructions. Claims above focus on expository prose plus the explicit pricing/statistic items that would need inline citation or rewriting.
